NEW FILE internal/store/sqlite/sync_emit.go with:
- emitEvent(ctx, tx, event) — inserts the row; called from inside every mutation's WithTx.
- nextLamport(ctx, tx) int64 — atomically reads meta.sync.lamport, increments, writes back, returns new value. Uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE-equivalent (SQLite SERIALIZABLE inside a tx is sufficient).
- currentVectorClock(ctx, tx) VectorClock — reads stored VC from meta key meta.sync.vector_clock (JSON map); empty map if absent.
- bumpAndPersistVC(ctx, tx, authorID) VectorClock — bumps local entry, writes back, returns new VC.
- machineHash(ctx, tx) string — reads/writes meta.sync.machine_hash; first call computes via internal/sync/clock.MachineHash() and persists.
- authorID(ctx) string — reads from context (set by command layer; falls back to 'cli' for tests).
- projectPrefix(ctx, tx, nodeID) string — derives from node_id.

WIRING (one mutation at a time, with a test for each):
| Mutation file:func | OpType | Payload |
| node_create.go:CreateNode | create_node | full Node serialization |
| node_update.go:UpdateNode | update_field, set_acceptance, set_prompt | one event per field changed; use set_acceptance for acceptance, set_prompt for prompt, update_field for everything else |
| transition.go:TransitionStatus | transition_status | {from, to} |
| claim.go:ClaimNode | claim | {agent_id, ttl_seconds} |
| claim.go:UnclaimNode | unclaim | {} |
| claim.go:ForceReclaimNode | claim | {agent_id, ttl_seconds, forced=true} |
| node_delete.go:DeleteNode | delete | {} (tombstone) |
| node_delete.go:UndeleteNode | NO EVENT — restore is idempotent against tombstone; document as local-only operation in comment |
| cancel.go:CancelNode | transition_status | {from, to=cancelled, reason} |
| dependency.go:AddDependency | link_dep | {depends_on, dep_type} |
| dependency.go:RemoveDependency | unlink_dep | {depends_on} |
| context.go:SetAnnotations | comment | {author_id, body} — NOTE: SetAnnotations currently does NOT use WithTx. WRAP it in WithTx in this ticket so the event is atomic with the annotation write. |
| store.go:UpdateProgress | NO EVENT — derived field; document. |

Asserts: op_type matches, payload deserializes to expected shape, lamport_clock > 0 and monotonically increasing, vector_clock contains author_id, author_machine_hash matches the value stored in meta, project_prefix matches node's project, sync_status='pending', wall_clock_ts is within 5s of now.

nodes.title: Queue size enforcement (sync.max_queue_size) + singleton pusher flock primitive
nodes.description: Two operational primitives: (1) refuse mutations when the local pending event queue exceeds the configured max; (2) flock-based singleton pusher lock so 10 concurrent agents on one machine produce exactly one pusher.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first, then implementation.

PART A — QUEUE SIZE ENFORCEMENT:
- Read sync.max_queue_size from meta (set in 15.2.2; default '0' = unlimited).
- New helper internal/store/sqlite/sync_emit.go:enforceQueueLimit(ctx, tx) error called BEFORE emitEvent in every wired mutation.
- If max > 0 and pending count >= max, return MTIX_SYNC_QUEUE_FULL structured error: 'mtix sync queue is full (N events pending; configured limit M). Run: mtix sync push --force, or raise the limit: mtix config set sync.max_queue_size 0'.
- Pending count = SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sync_events WHERE sync_status='pending'.
- New error type model.ErrSyncQueueFull (sentinel) so callers can errors.Is.

CONFIGURATION:
- New CLI subcommand: mtix config set sync.max_queue_size <N> (writes to meta).
- New CLI subcommand: mtix config get sync.max_queue_size (reads from meta).
- These reuse any existing config-set/config-get plumbing if it exists; otherwise a minimal cobra command in cmd/mtix/config.go.

PART B — SINGLETON PUSHER FLOCK:
- New file internal/sync/pushlock/pushlock_unix.go (build constraint: !windows).
- New file internal/sync/pushlock/pushlock_windows.go (build constraint: windows).
- Function: AcquirePushLock(mtixDir string) (*PushLock, error) — opens .mtix/data/sync.push.lock with O_CREATE|O_RDWR mode 0600; calls syscall.Flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB) on Unix; LockFileEx on Windows.
- On contention: returns ErrPushLockHeld sentinel.
- Method: (l *PushLock) Release() error — Flock(LOCK_UN) + Close on Unix; UnlockFileEx + Close on Windows.
- The lock is auto-released on process exit (kernel guarantee). Release() is for graceful explicit cases.
- Mirror — do NOT import — internal/service/flock_*.go's pattern. Putting the lock under internal/sync/pushlock keeps the new sync tree self-contained.

NO actual pusher logic in this ticket. The pusher itself lands in MTIX-15.7. This ticket only provides the lock primitive.

TESTS:
PART A:
- TestQueueLimit_Unlimited (max=0): emit 100 events, all succeed.
- TestQueueLimit_Reached: set max=5, emit 5, the 6th mutation returns ErrSyncQueueFull with the documented message.
- TestQueueLimit_AfterPush: set max=5, emit 5, mark first 3 as pushed, emit 3 more (total 5 pending), succeed.
- TestQueueLimit_Reentry: same DB, two goroutines emit; both serialized by tx; neither sees stale count.
- TestConfig_SetGetMaxQueueSize: round-trip via the new CLI subcommands.

PART B:
- TestPushLock_SingleAcquire: one goroutine acquires; second concurrent acquire returns ErrPushLockHeld.
- TestPushLock_AfterRelease: acquire, release, second acquire succeeds.
- TestPushLock_TenConcurrent: spawn 10 goroutines; assert exactly one acquires; the other 9 receive ErrPushLockHeld; the holder releases; one of the others can then acquire.
- TestPushLock_AutoReleaseOnExit (subprocess test): child acquires the lock then os.Exit(0); parent immediately acquires it.
- Cross-platform: tests run on linux + macOS (CI matrix); windows test stubbed if CI does not run windows yet.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Queue limit enforced atomically with mutation.
- ErrSyncQueueFull message matches FR-18 exactly.
- Singleton lock test: 10 concurrent goroutines, exactly one wins.
- 90% coverage on pushlock + queue-limit code.
- Race-detector clean.

DEPENDS ON: MTIX-15.2.3 (uses emitEvent in tests; emitEvent must exist).

nodes.title: Hub transport (PushEvents/PullEvents API + TLS + retry)
nodes.description: PG client wrapper exposing PushEvents(events) -> accepted_ids, conflicts; PullEvents(since_clock) -> events. pgxpool with sane defaults. Statement timeout + connection timeout. Exponential backoff for retries. TLS verify-full default; refuse weaker except --insecure-tls localhost. DSN sourcing: MTIX_SYNC_DSN env var only OR .mtix/secrets file; refuse tracked config. Schema migration on first connect with PG advisory lock. SQL injection regression test (port from MTIX-9.1 pattern).
nodes.prompt:
Implement the hub transport layer: PG client wrapper that exposes PushEvents and PullEvents over the connection pool. Build on MTIX-15.1 design and MTIX-15.2 schema.

API SHAPE:
- PushEvents(ctx, []Event) (accepted_event_ids []string, conflicts []ConflictDescriptor, err error)
- PullEvents(ctx, since_lamport int64, limit int) ([]Event, has_more bool, err error)
- Both go through pgxpool with sane defaults: max_conns=8, conn_lifetime=30m, statement_timeout=10s, healthcheck_period=1m.
- Exponential backoff on transient errors (network, statement_timeout): base=200ms, factor=2, max=10s, max_attempts=5. Permanent errors (auth, schema mismatch) fail immediately.

TLS DEFAULTS:
- sslmode=verify-full default. Refuse weaker (verify-ca, prefer, disable) UNLESS --insecure-tls flag set AND host resolves to loopback.
- SSLROOTCERT support (NEW MEDIUM): honor MTIX_SYNC_SSLROOTCERT env var and DSN sslrootcert= parameter. Document that managed PG providers (Supabase, Neon, RDS) often need this. Test with a self-signed cert chain.

DSN SOURCING:
- MTIX_SYNC_DSN env var only OR .mtix/secrets file (mode 0600, gitignored, validate at load time).
- Refuse to load DSN from any tracked YAML/JSON config file (fail-closed at config-load).
- Test: place DSN in .mtix/config.yaml, expect refusal with structured error MTIX_SYNC_DSN_IN_TRACKED_FILE.

SCHEMA MIGRATION ON FIRST CONNECT:
- Acquire PG advisory lock pg_advisory_xact_lock(hash('mtix_sync_migration')) inside the migration tx.
- 10 concurrent CLIs first-connect: only one runs the migration, others see the result.
- Concurrent-migration test from 15.2 must pass against this transport.

NEW REQUIREMENTS FROM SECURITY/SAFETY REVIEW:
1. SCHEMA VALIDATION WITH SIZE LIMITS (HIGH) - Validate every incoming event in PushEvents BEFORE applying:
   - event_id is valid UUID v7.
   - op_type is one of the 12 documented enum values.
   - payload is valid JSON, <=64KB serialized, no nested depth >10.
   - wall_clock_ts is int64, non-negative, <= now+24h (reject SYNC_TIMESTAMP_FUTURE per 15.1).
   - lamport_clock is int64, non-negative.
   - vector_clock is a flat map[string]int64, <=100 entries, each value <2^53 (overflow protection - also see 15.11).
   - author_id matches /^[a-z0-9_-]{1,64}/.
   - Reject malformed events with structured error MTIX_SYNC_EVENT_INVALID + which field failed. NEVER apply a partially-validated event.
2. REJECT FUTURE TIMESTAMPS (HIGH) - Hub-side check: if any event in batch has wall_clock_ts > server now+24h, reject ENTIRE batch with one error citing the offending event_id. Test with skew of +25h, +30d, +1y.
3. PARTIAL-MIGRATION RECOVERY TEST (MEDIUM) - Chaos test: kill the CLI process mid-migration (between two ALTER TABLE statements). Restart. Verify: advisory lock is released by PG (transaction rollback), next CLI re-runs the WHOLE migration cleanly, no half-applied schema. Use a hook in the test that pauses between statements and SIGKILLs the process.

SQL INJECTION REGRESSION:
- Port the MTIX-9.1 attack-pattern test to the PG transport.
- Every parameter passes through pgx bound-parameter API; string concatenation of user input into SQL is forbidden. Add a golangci lint rule (or a grep-based pre-commit) to enforce.

ACCEPTANCE:
- TLS verify-full enforced (test: connect with verify-ca fails closed).
- DSN refused from tracked config file (test passes).
- 10 concurrent first-connects: exactly one runs migration, advisory-lock observable in pg_locks.
- Schema validator rejects all 7 malformed-event classes with structured errors.
- Partial-migration kill-9 test passes.
- SQL injection regression passes.
- 90% coverage, race-detector clean.

NEW PACKAGE internal/store/postgres/transport with:

1. CONNECTION + POOL
- New(ctx, dsn, opts) (*Pool, error) — parses DSN, enforces TLS posture, opens pgxpool.
- Pool.Close() — closes the pool.
- Pool.HealthCheck(ctx) error — pings PG.
- Defaults: max_conns=8, conn_lifetime=30m, statement_timeout=10s, healthcheck_period=1m.

2. DSN SOURCING (Source function in same package)
- DSN MUST come from MTIX_SYNC_DSN env var OR .mtix/secrets file (mode 0600 enforced; gitignore check is best-effort).
- Refuse if .mtix/config.yaml or any tracked YAML/JSON contains 'sync.dsn'/'pg.dsn' key — return MTIX_SYNC_DSN_IN_TRACKED_FILE structured error.

3. TLS POSTURE

- Refuse weaker (verify-ca, prefer, allow, disable) UNLESS opts.InsecureTLS=true AND host resolves to loopback (127.0.0.1, ::1, localhost).

- TestTLS_RefusesWeakerWithoutInsecureFlag: DSN with sslmode=disable, opts.InsecureTLS=false, refused.

- TestMigrate_FreshHubAppliesAllSchema: empty PG, Migrate runs, all 7 SQL files apply, expected tables present.

- TestMigrate_ConcurrentSingleFlight: 10 goroutines call Migrate, exactly one runs (assert via PG transaction count or by inserting a marker in the migration), rest wait, all see committed result.

nodes.title: PushEvents + PullEvents API + retry/backoff
nodes.description: The hub-side push/pull endpoints. Validates events via 15.3.2, runs them in a tx via the 15.3.1 pool, returns accepted_ids and conflict descriptors.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first.

API:
- type ConflictDescriptor struct { EventID, ConflictingEventID, NodeID, FieldName string }
- (p *Pool) PushEvents(ctx, events []*model.SyncEvent) (acceptedIDs []string, conflicts []ConflictDescriptor, err error)
- (p *Pool) PullEvents(ctx, sinceLamport int64, limit int) (events []*model.SyncEvent, hasMore bool, err error)

PUSH semantics:
- Validate the entire batch first (via 15.3.2 ValidateBatch); on any failure, reject the whole batch with structured error MTIX_SYNC_EVENT_INVALID and the failing event_id+field.
- INSERT all valid events into sync_events using a single tx with ON CONFLICT (event_id) DO NOTHING (idempotent re-push).
- Conflict detection: for each event, compare against existing events on (node_id, field_name) with overlapping vector clocks. If concurrent (per VectorClock.Concurrent), append a ConflictDescriptor. (Full LWW resolution is a 15.5 concern; this ticket just IDs conflicts.)
- Return accepted_ids = the events that landed (deduplicated against ON CONFLICT).

PULL semantics:
- SELECT * FROM sync_events WHERE lamport_clock > ? ORDER BY lamport_clock LIMIT ?+1.
- If returned count > limit, hasMore = true and trim.
- Return events in lamport order.

RETRY:
- Wrap PG calls in retryWithBackoff(maxAttempts=5, base=200ms, factor=2, max=10s).
- Retry only transient errors: pgx.ErrTxClosed, network errors, statement_timeout.
- Permanent errors (auth, schema mismatch, validation) fail immediately.

TESTS (skip if MTIX_PG_TEST_DSN unset):
- TestPushEvents_HappyPath: 5 valid events, all accepted.
- TestPushEvents_RejectsEntireBatchOnInvalid: 4 valid + 1 invalid, all rejected.
- TestPushEvents_IdempotentOnRepush: push 3, push same 3 again, accepted_ids on second push is empty.
- TestPushEvents_DetectsConcurrentEdits: push event A from author1, push event B from author2 with concurrent VC for same field, conflict descriptor returned.
- TestPullEvents_FromZero: pull from lamport=0, get all.
- TestPullEvents_FromHighWaterMark: push 10, pull from lamport=5, get 5.
- TestPullEvents_HasMoreFlag: push 20, pull with limit=10, hasMore=true.
- TestPullEvents_OrderedByLamport: push out-of-order events, pull, returned in lamport order.
- TestRetry_RetriesTransientThenSucceeds: inject transient error (pgx network), assert retry kicks in.
- TestRetry_FailsImmediatelyOnPermanent: inject auth error, assert no retry.
- TestRetry_BackoffBoundsRespected: simulate failures, assert delays ~ 200ms, 400ms, 800ms, 1600ms, 3200ms (with jitter tolerance).

ACCEPTANCE:
- PushEvents idempotent under repush.
- PullEvents respects lamport order and hasMore.
- Retry only on transient.
- Conflict descriptors returned (full resolution in 15.5).
- 90% coverage.
nodes.acceptance: PushEvents/PullEvents work end-to-end against test PG; idempotent repush; retry-only-on-transient; conflict descriptors returned; 90% coverage.

nodes.title: SQL injection regression + DSN hygiene sweep + panic-message redaction
nodes.description: Cross-cutting security: SQL injection regression test ported from MTIX-9.1; sweep that proves DSN never appears in any observable output; top-level defer-recover that runs panic values through RedactDSN.

TDD: tests first.

PART A — SQL INJECTION REGRESSION:
- Port MTIX-9.1 attack patterns (e.g., ');DROP TABLE--, ' OR '1'='1) to PushEvents inputs.
- For each attack pattern, populate event fields (NodeID, AuthorID, Payload) with the pattern, push, verify:
  1. Either rejected by the validator (via regex), or
  2. Accepted as a literal string and stored verbatim with no SQL side-effect (e.g., target table still queryable).
- Test passes when no attack pattern causes data loss or unauthorized state change.

PART B — DSN HYGIENE SWEEP:
- New test TestDSN_NeverInObservableOutput: inject a synthetic DSN with a unique sentinel password ('PASSWORD_LEAK_SENTINEL_xyz123'), exercise the full transport surface (open pool, push events, pull events, force errors via bad SQL), and grep stdout, stderr, log buffer, MCP tool output for the sentinel string. Test fails if the sentinel appears anywhere.

PART C — PANIC-MESSAGE REDACTION:

- Add a top-level defer-recover wrapper helper RecoverWithRedaction(logger) that callers can use in goroutine entry points. Logs the redacted panic value at ERROR.
- Test: deliberately panic with a real DSN in the panic value; verify the printed/logged output contains the redacted form, NOT the original DSN.

PART D — STATIC LINT FOR RAW SQL CONCAT:

- TestDSN_NeverInLogs
- TestDSN_NeverInErrors
- TestDSN_NeverInPanic

nodes.title: Event apply engine (idempotent replay + node_type canonicalization)
nodes.description: IdempotentApply(event) function applies event to local nodes table within transaction. Reject duplicates (event_id seen). Update vector_clock on apply. Handle all 12 operation types (create, update_field, transition_status, claim, delete, etc.). On apply: enforce NodeTypeForDepth canonicalization (ignores any non-canonical node_type in the event payload, matches MTIX-7/12 contract). Tests: replay same event twice (idempotent); apply create-then-update sequence; apply out-of-order events with vector clock reordering; synthetic event with wrong node_type for depth applies canonical.
nodes.prompt:
Implement the IdempotentApply(event) function: take a single sync event and apply it to the local nodes table within a single SQLite transaction.

CONTRACT:
- IdempotentApply(ctx, tx, event) error
- Reject duplicate event_id silently (return nil, log debug). Detect via SELECT 1 FROM applied_events WHERE event_id=?.
- Update target node's vector_clock entry for event.author_id.
- Update local lamport clock = max(local, event.lamport_clock).
- Append to applied_events table (event_id, applied_at, applied_by_lamport).
- Handle all 12 op types from MTIX-15.1 design: create_node, update_field, transition_status, claim, unclaim, defer, comment, link_dep, unlink_dep, delete (tombstone), set_acceptance, set_prompt.

NODE_TYPE CANONICALIZATION (preserved from original):
- On every apply that touches node_type: ignore any non-canonical value in the event payload, ALWAYS use NodeTypeForDepth(node.depth). Matches MTIX-7/12 contract - a peer with a buggy local mtix cannot poison the hub's hierarchy.
- Test: synthetic event with depth=0 + node_type='story' applies as 'epic'.

ORIGINAL TESTS (preserved):
- Replay same event twice: idempotent, second is a no-op.
- Apply create-then-update sequence: final state matches direct mutation.
- Apply out-of-order events with vector clock reordering: state converges per LWW.
- Synthetic event with wrong node_type for depth applies canonical node_type.

NEW REQUIREMENTS FROM SECURITY/SAFETY REVIEW:
1. PROPERTY-BASED TEST FOR REPLAY DETERMINISM (HIGH) - Use github.com/leanovate/gopter or similar. Generate a random sequence of 100 events across 10 nodes. Apply in order A. Apply the same sequence in shuffled order B (where shuffling preserves vector-clock causal ordering). Final state MUST be byte-identical (compare nodes-table content_hash). Run with at least 1000 random seeds in CI.
2. CHAOS TEST FOR KILL-9 MID-APPLY (HIGH) - Test harness: spawn a child process that calls IdempotentApply on a known event, SIGKILL it after a configurable delay (parameterize 0ms to 50ms in 5ms steps). After kill, restart, verify either (a) the event is fully applied AND in applied_events, OR (b) the event is fully unapplied AND not in applied_events. Never partial. This proves the SQLite transaction boundary holds.
3. EVENT REPLAY AGAINST EMPTY DB (no event_id duplicate suppression bypass) - verify that an event with op_type=delete on a non-existent node is a no-op (does not create a phantom tombstone). Tombstones only mark deletion of previously-existing nodes.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All 12 op types covered with table-driven tests.
- Property test (1000 seeds) passes - final state determinism.
- Chaos test (10 kill points x 100 iterations) passes - atomicity.
- node_type canonicalization regression passes.
- 90% coverage on apply engine, race-detector clean.

nodes.title: Apply engine: replay determinism property test (1000 seeds)
nodes.description: Property-based test per FR-18.9 / SYNC-DESIGN section 8: generate random sequences of events; apply in order A; apply the same sequence shuffled (preserving causal order); assert final state byte-identical via content_hash. 1000 random seeds in CI.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: this ticket IS the test.

NEW FILE internal/store/sqlite/sync_apply_property_test.go:
- TestApply_ReplayDeterminismProperty(t *testing.T):
  - For each of N seeds (N=1000 in CI; N=50 in fast local runs via -short flag):
    1. Generate a random sequence of K events (K=100 in CI, K=20 in -short) across M nodes (M=10).

- Use math/rand with a deterministic seed derived from the test iteration index so failures are reproducible.

- Author pool: 3 simulated authors (alice, bob, carol).

SHUFFLE CONSTRAINT:
- Two events e1, e2 from the same author MUST appear in lamport order.
- Two events from different authors are reorderable.
- Implementation: bucket events by author, then interleave the buckets randomly.

- Test passes 1000 iterations in CI.
- On failure: print the seed, the original order, the shuffled order, and the diverging node IDs for reproducibility.

nodes.title: Conflict detection (vector clocks) + resolution (LWW with surface)
nodes.description: Vector clock comparison detects concurrent vs causally-ordered events. Three resolution classes: disjoint fields (no conflict, both apply), same field (LWW by timestamp, deterministic tie-break by author hash), delete vs update (tombstone wins). All conflicts logged to sync_conflicts table on hub AND local .mtix/conflicts.log. User can override via mtix sync conflicts resolve. Test matrix: every conflict class with deterministic outcomes; property test for LWW transitivity.
nodes.prompt:
Implement conflict detection (vector clocks) and conflict resolution (Last-Write-Wins with surfacing) on both the local apply engine (15.4) and the hub transport (15.3).

DETECTION:
- Vector clock comparison: event A is concurrent with event B iff A.vc <= B.vc is false AND B.vc <= A.vc is false. Apply 15.4-style causal ordering when one strictly dominates; trigger conflict resolution otherwise.

RESOLUTION CLASSES (deterministic, testable matrix):
- Disjoint fields: both events touch different fields of the same node - both apply, no conflict logged.
- Same field: Last-Write-Wins by lamport_clock first, wall_clock_ts as deterministic tie-break, author_id hash as final tie-break (lower hash wins). NO randomness.
- Delete vs update: tombstone always wins (delete is monotonic; once deleted, stays deleted).

LOGGING:
- Every non-trivial resolution (i.e. anything that drops a value) logs a row to sync_conflicts (hub) AND a line to .mtix/conflicts.log (local).
- Local log entry: timestamp, node_id, field_name, kept_value_event_id, dropped_value_event_id, resolution_rule.
- Hub log entry: same plus author_id pair.

USER OVERRIDE:
- mtix sync conflicts list (15.7 implements CLI surface).
- mtix sync conflicts resolve <conflict_id> --action keep-local|keep-remote|both-renumbered creates a new event that supersedes the LWW outcome. Recorded as resolution=manual in sync_conflicts.

ORIGINAL TESTS (preserved):
- Test matrix: every conflict class with deterministic outcomes.
- Property test for LWW transitivity.

NEW REQUIREMENTS FROM SECURITY/SAFETY REVIEW:
1. CROSS-MACHINE PROPERTY TEST (HIGH) - Spin up two ephemeral SQLite stores in-process simulating two laptops. Generate random concurrent mutation sequences (100 events each, overlapping node set). Push to a shared in-memory hub. Pull back to both. Assert both laptops converge to byte-identical state (compare content_hash on every node). 1000 seeds. This is the strongest convergence guarantee we can express without a real PG.
2. BATCH RESOLUTION UX (MEDIUM) - When >50 conflicts exist for one project, mtix sync conflicts list shows a banner: 'X conflicts grouped across Y nodes. Use mtix sync conflicts resolve --batch <node_id> to accept LWW for all fields of one node, or --batch-all to accept LWW for everything (DANGEROUS).' Implement --batch <node_id> and --batch-all flags. --batch-all requires a confirmation prompt with the count of values that will be dropped.
3. AGENT-FRIENDLY CONFLICT SURFACE (carry-forward from 15.1) - When mtix context <node_id> is called on a node with unresolved conflicts, append a CONFLICT block listing each conflicting field with both candidate values. Agents are instructed (via AGENTS.md) to NOT silently choose; they should mtix_comment requesting human resolution.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Conflict matrix table-driven test passes (every (op_a, op_b, field) combination).
- Cross-machine property test (1000 seeds) passes.
- Batch resolution flags work; --batch-all confirmation prompt cannot be bypassed without --yes.
- mtix context surfaces CONFLICT block on conflicted nodes.
- 90% coverage, race-detector clean.

    resolution TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (resolution IN ('lww','tombstone','manual')),
    resolved_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    resolved_by TEXT
  Indexes: (node_id), (resolved_at).
- Add column hint to sync_events: nothing new; existing columns suffice.

BEHAVIOR:
1. When applying update_field / set_acceptance / set_prompt:
   - Look up highest-lamport prior event for the same (node_id, field) in sync_events local mirror (rows with sync_status IN ('pushed','applied')).
   - 'field' for update_field comes from payload.field_name; for set_acceptance/set_prompt, the op_type IS the field.
   - Compare lamport_clock; tie-break by wall_clock_ts; final tie-break by author_machine_hash (lower wins, lex compare).
   - If incoming WINS: apply normally + record conflict (when applicable).
   - If incoming LOSES: skip the SQL update, record conflict, do NOT bump applied_events (the event was 'observed' but its mutation was overridden).
2. Pulled events that we apply MUST be mirrored into local sync_events with sync_status='applied' so LWW lookups work. (This is the MTIX-15.7 pull loop's job, but the schema and helper APIs land here.)
3. .mtix/conflicts.log JSON lines: {timestamp, conflict_id, winner, loser, node_id, field, resolution}.

- detectAndResolveLWW(ctx, tx, e) (winnerEventID string, loser bool, err error) — returns the winning event_id and whether the incoming event lost.
- recordLocalConflict(ctx, tx, winner, loser *model.SyncEvent, field string) — INSERT into sync_conflicts.

DELETE VS UPDATE: already handled via 'WHERE deleted_at IS NULL' in applyUpdateField etc. After a tombstone applies, subsequent updates silently fail with ErrNotFound. Document this in code as the intended behavior; don't change.

- TestApplyLWW_SameFieldLowerLamportLoses: e1{lamport=10} applied; e2{lamport=5} arrives; SQL not updated; conflict recorded with loser=e2.
- TestApplyLWW_SameLamportTieBreakByWallClockTS: e1{lamport=5, ts=100}, e2{lamport=5, ts=200} -> e2 wins.

nodes.title: Divergent-history detection + reconciliation tooling
nodes.description: First-sync check: query sync_projects for prefix; if exists, compare first_event_hash with local first event. Mismatch -> refuse with structured error + four options: --discard-local (drop local, take hub state), --rename-to NEWPREFIX (rewrite local node IDs under new prefix), --import-as PARENT-ID (Charlies nodes become descendants of new hub node), --dry-run (preview). Audit trail of every renamed event (RECONCILE_START, RENAME_NODE, RECONCILE_DONE). Local id-rename-map.json for agent consultation post-reconciliation. Tests: full Charlie-joins-team scenario with all four resolution paths.
nodes.prompt:
Implement divergent-history detection and the four reconciliation paths.

FIRST-SYNC CHECK:
- On mtix sync clone OR first mtix sync push: query sync_projects WHERE prefix=local.project_prefix.
- If row exists, compare hub.first_event_hash with local first event's content hash.
- If they differ: refuse with structured error MTIX_SYNC_DIVERGENT_HISTORY containing the four resolution-path option summaries.

FOUR RESOLUTION PATHS:
1. --discard-local: Drop local nodes table, clear sync_events, run a fresh clone. Unrecoverable - prompt y/N (require --yes to skip prompt).
2. --rename-to NEWPREFIX: Rewrite all local node IDs from <oldprefix>-N to <newprefix>-N atomically; then push with the new prefix. Must register a new sync_projects row for NEWPREFIX. Both prefixes coexist on the hub.
3. --import-as PARENT-ID: Charlie's nodes become descendants of an existing hub node. Re-parent the entire local tree under PARENT-ID; push as additions. Existing local IDs are renumbered to fit under PARENT-ID's namespace.
4. --dry-run: Preview only. Show counts: how many nodes would be renamed/dropped/re-parented; what new IDs would be assigned; estimated wall-clock for the reconciliation.

AUDIT TRAIL:
- Every reconciliation emits structured audit events: RECONCILE_START (chosen path, counts), RENAME_NODE (old_id, new_id) per node, RECONCILE_DONE (final stats).
- Persist .mtix/id-rename-map.json so post-reconciliation agent sessions can look up 'where did MTIX-3.1 go'. Map format: {'old_id': 'new_id', ...}.

ORIGINAL TESTS (preserved):
- Full Charlie-joins-team scenario with all four resolution paths.

NEW REQUIREMENTS FROM SECURITY/SAFETY REVIEW:
1. RECONCILIATION ATOMICITY TEST (HIGH) - Test harness must inject a synthetic failure mid-reconciliation:
   - For --rename-to: fail the SQLite UPDATE on node #5 of 10. Expected outcome: ALL 10 renames roll back, RECONCILE_START audit event still present (it MUST be), RECONCILE_DONE absent, .mtix/id-rename-map.json absent OR contains nothing past the failure point and is marked partial=true. Critically: re-running the reconcile after the failure starts from clean state.
   - For --import-as: fail the hub PushEvents call on event #5 of 20. Expected outcome: local nodes still re-parented, but local sync_status set to 'pending' so the next push completes. NO duplicate events on the hub.
   - For --discard-local: fail the DROP TABLE on the second table. Expected outcome: schema is left in a known-bad state with an explicit MTIX_RECONCILE_ABORTED error file at .mtix/.reconcile.aborted; subsequent mtix calls refuse to run until user runs mtix sync reconcile --resume or --abort-cleanup.
2. RENAME COLLISION CHECK - Before --rename-to NEWPREFIX, query the hub for any project with prefix=NEWPREFIX. If found, refuse with MTIX_RECONCILE_PREFIX_COLLISION listing the existing project's node count. Test passes when the collision is detected before any local mutation.
3. DRY-RUN COMPLETENESS - Test that --dry-run produces output identical to the actual run's audit events (minus side effects). Diff the predicted plan against the executed plan.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All four resolution paths work end-to-end (Charlie scenario test).
- Atomicity test passes for all three failure-mode scenarios.
- Prefix collision detected before any local mutation.
- Dry-run plan matches actual executed plan.
- Audit events recorded for every reconciliation step.
- .mtix/id-rename-map.json correctly persisted (or absent on failure).
- 90% coverage, race-detector clean.

nodes.title: Divergent history detection + local sync_projects schema + first_event_hash computation

nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first.

SCHEMA: add local sync_projects table mirroring hub schema (003_sync_projects.sql):
- project_prefix TEXT PRIMARY KEY
- first_event_hash TEXT NOT NULL
- created_at TEXT NOT NULL
- schema_version INTEGER DEFAULT 1
- last_seen_cli_version TEXT

Add via additive CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS — same approach as 15.5.1's sync_conflicts.

FIRST EVENT HASH:
- ComputeFirstEventHash(event *model.SyncEvent) string — SHA-256 over canonical serialization of:
  - project_prefix
  - node_id
  - op_type (string form)
  - payload (canonical JSON: keys sorted)
  - lamport_clock (decimal string)
  - vector_clock (canonical JSON via VectorClock.MarshalJSON which is deterministic per 15.2.1)
  - author_id
  - author_machine_hash
- Returns hex-encoded 64-char string.
- DOES NOT include wall_clock_ts (varies across replicas) or event_id (unique per emit).
- Determinism: two replicas computing the hash for the same event get the same string.

DETECTION API:
- DetectDivergentHistory(localPrefix, localHash, hubPrefix, hubHash string) error
  - If hubPrefix != localPrefix or hubHash == "": no conflict (different project or hub fresh)
  - If localHash == hubHash: no conflict (same project, matching history)
  - Otherwise: return wrapped model.ErrSyncDivergentHistory with the four-option summary text
- New sentinel: model.ErrSyncDivergentHistory in internal/model/errors.go

LOCAL FIRST_EVENT_HASH STORAGE:
- New helper getOrComputeLocalFirstEventHash(ctx, store) (prefix, hash string, err error):
  - Read meta.sync.first_event_hash if cached (already computed once)
  - Else: SELECT first event from sync_events ORDER BY lamport_clock LIMIT 1
  - Compute hash via ComputeFirstEventHash, cache in meta, return
- Add 'meta.sync.first_event_hash' INSERT OR IGNORE to schemaSQL (default empty)
- Add 'meta.sync.project_prefix' too — derived from the first node's id when first event lands

- TestDetectDivergentHistory_HubFresh: hub hash empty, returns nil.
- TestDetectDivergentHistory_Mismatch: returns wrapped ErrSyncDivergentHistory + four-option summary in error message.
- TestSchema_LocalSyncProjectsTable: fresh DB has the table with expected columns.
- TestGetOrComputeLocalFirstEventHash_FirstCallComputesAndCaches: writes to meta.sync.first_event_hash.
- TestGetOrComputeLocalFirstEventHash_SubsequentCallsReadFromCache: doesn't re-scan sync_events.

- DetectDivergentHistory returns structured error with the four-option summary

nodes.title: CLI commands (sync init/clone/push/pull/status/daemon, conflicts, doctor)
nodes.description: 9 commands: mtix sync init DSN (first user creates hub + registers project), mtix sync clone DSN (joiner pulls all events into fresh local SQLite), mtix sync push (explicit, auto by default), mtix sync pull (explicit), mtix sync status (pending/pushed/conflicted counts + both sentinels: file sync + hub sync), mtix sync daemon (opt-in background pull every 30s), mtix sync conflicts list, mtix sync conflicts resolve ID --action keep-local|keep-remote|both-renumbered, mtix sync doctor (PG reachable, schema current, queue draining, no orphan events). All commands warn-and-skip on PG unreachable (do NOT block code pushes when used from a hook). Note for Docker MCP container users: configure MTIX_SYNC_DSN as a Docker MCP secret so the containerized mtix can be a sync participant.
nodes.prompt:
Implement 9 new CLI commands for the sync subsystem. All commands MUST warn-and-skip (exit 0 with WARN line on stderr) when PG is unreachable, so a git pre-push hook never blocks code pushes when sync is degraded. NEVER fail-hard for transient PG errors in the hook context (env var MTIX_SYNC_HOOK=1 enables this mode).

COMMANDS:
1. mtix sync init <DSN>
   - First user creates the hub (runs schema migration via 15.3 advisory lock).
   - Registers project (writes sync_projects row with first_event_hash from the local first event).
   - Stores DSN in .mtix/secrets (mode 0600, gitignored). Refuses if .mtix/secrets is git-tracked.
   - Output: success line + 'NEXT: share the DSN with your team via your secrets manager (NOT git).'
2. mtix sync clone <DSN>
   - Joiner: pulls all events into a fresh local SQLite. Refuses if local has any events (use 'mtix sync init --discard-local' instead).
3. mtix sync push [--force]
   - Default: opportunistic (auto-runs in background after every mutation if not running already, via singleton pusher from 15.2).
   - --force ignores singleton lock (debug only, with warning).
4. mtix sync pull [--limit N]
   - Pulls events since meta.sync.last_pulled_clock; applies via 15.4 IdempotentApply.
5. mtix sync status
   - Shows: pending count, pushed count, conflicted count, BOTH sentinels (file-sync + hub-sync), hub reachable y/n, last successful push/pull timestamps.
6. mtix sync daemon (opt-in)
   - Background pull every 30s. Writes PID to .mtix/sync.daemon.pid. Idempotent start (existing PID = no-op).
   - Supports systemd unit + launchd plist generation via mtix sync daemon install.
7. mtix sync conflicts list
   - 15.5 backend; supports --batch flag display.
8. mtix sync conflicts resolve <id> --action keep-local|keep-remote|both-renumbered
   - 15.5 backend. Refuses if id is unknown. Logs the resolution.
9. mtix sync doctor
   - Runs 5 health checks: PG reachable, schema current, queue draining (no events older than 1h in pending), no orphan events (every applied_event has matching node OR tombstone), DSN file mode is 0600.
   - Exit 0 if all pass; exit 2 if any fail; structured JSON output with --json.

DOCKER MCP CONTAINER NOTE:
- For users running mtix in the Docker MCP container: configure MTIX_SYNC_DSN as a Docker MCP secret. Document this in the workflow for safety-critical teams (15.12 docs).

NEW REQUIREMENTS FROM SECURITY/SAFETY REVIEW:
1. STREAMED CLONE WITH PROGRESS BAR (HIGH) - mtix sync clone for large projects (>10K events) MUST batch the pull at 1000 events per request. Display a progress bar (events_pulled / total_events). Allow --resume after interruption: write meta.sync.clone.checkpoint after every batch; resume from the checkpoint if .mtix exists with a partial clone marker.
2. HUB BACKUP / DR PROCEDURE (HIGH) - Add a new command mtix sync backup --output FILE that calls pg_dump for the mtix-owned tables (sync_events, sync_conflicts, sync_projects, applied_events, audit_log) into a portable SQL dump. Store rotation/retention is the user's job; mtix only provides the backup tool. Document the corresponding restore command (psql < FILE) in the workflow doc 15.12. Add a test that round-trips a hub through backup -> drop schema -> restore -> verify event_count + first_event_hash match.
3. CLI VERSION COMPATIBILITY CHECK (HIGH) - On every PushEvents/PullEvents request, send the local mtix version. The hub stores the highest seen version per project in sync_projects.last_seen_cli_version. mtix sync doctor warns if local version < last_seen_cli_version - 1 minor (i.e. you are >1 minor behind your team). The check is advisory, not blocking.
4. DSN HYGIENE REGRESSION - Test sweep: run all 9 commands against a deliberately misconfigured environment (DSN in tracked file, secrets file mode 0644, etc.). Each MUST fail-closed with the appropriate structured error before any network call.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All 9 commands implemented with --help text and integration tests.
- mtix sync doctor returns correct exit codes for healthy/unhealthy states.
- mtix sync clone with progress bar + resume works on a 50K-event simulated project.
- mtix sync backup round-trip test passes.
- CLI version mismatch warning appears in mtix sync doctor when applicable.
- Hook mode (MTIX_SYNC_HOOK=1) never fails-hard on transient PG errors.
- DSN hygiene regression sweep passes for all 9 commands.
- 90% coverage, race-detector clean.

nodes.title: mtix sync push + pull
nodes.description: Explicit push and pull commands using the 15.3 transport. Push transmits pending events; pull applies remote events via IdempotentApply. Hook mode (MTIX_SYNC_HOOK=1) warn-and-skips on transient PG errors so git pre-push hooks never block code pushes.

- mtix sync pull [--limit N] — calls transport.PullEvents from meta.sync.last_pulled_clock; for each event runs IdempotentApply; on success updates last_pulled_clock to max returned lamport.

nodes.title: Add outgoing webhook support for state change notifications
nodes.description: Generic webhook system for push-based notifications. When a node changes state, mtix fires an HTTP POST to configured URLs. Enables event-driven sync with external tools without mtix knowing anything about Jira/Linear/GitHub Issues.
nodes.prompt:
Implementation requirements from security and performance audit:

Config (.mtix/config.yaml):
  webhooks:
    - url: https://example.com/webhook
      secret: hmac-secret
      events: [node.created, node.done, node.cancelled, node.blocked]
      payload: minimal  # or full

Core requirements:
1. ASYNC delivery — dispatch from event broadcaster goroutine, NEVER inside a DB transaction (WH-01)
2. Minimal default payload: {event, node_id, status, project, timestamp}. Full payload (includes title, assignee) only when payload: full is set (WH-02)
3. HMAC-SHA256 signature in X-Mtix-Signature header using per-webhook secret (WH-03)
4. 100ms debounce window — collect events, send as batch array to prevent bulk operation floods (WH-04)
5. Config-file only — not API-configurable (WH-05)
6. Warn at startup if webhook URL is non-HTTPS and non-localhost (WH-06)
7. 5-second timeout per delivery
8. 3 retries with exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 25s), then drop with warning log
9. HTTP client with connection pooling (reuse connections to same URL)

Files: internal/service/config.go (webhook config struct), internal/service/broadcaster.go (webhook dispatch), internal/service/webhook.go (new — HTTP client, HMAC, batching), cmd/mtix/root.go (config loading and validation).
nodes.acceptance: 1. Webhook fires on configured events. 2. Payload matches configured mode (minimal/full). 3. X-Mtix-Signature header present and verifiable. 4. Slow endpoint does not block mtix operations. 5. Bulk decompose (50 children) produces batched webhook, not 50 individual calls. 6. Non-HTTPS non-localhost URL produces startup warning. 7. Failed delivery retries 3 times then logs warning. 8. Config-file only — no API endpoint to add webhooks. 9. Test coverage for delivery, HMAC, batching, timeout, retry.

nodes.title: Skip redundant auto-import via meta table export hash
nodes.description: Auto-import triggers on every command when tasks.json hash differs from stored hash. With multiple agents on the same machine, each mutation triggers an export (hash changes) which causes all other agents to do a full ImportModeReplace — a thundering herd problem. Fix: store last export hash in the meta table. If file hash matches last export hash, the file came from our own export — skip import. External changes (git pull) produce a different hash and import correctly.
nodes.prompt:
TDD approach — write failing test first.

TEST (in internal/service/sync_service_test.go):
TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenFileMatchesOwnExport: Create a sync service, insert nodes, call AutoExport (writes tasks.json + sets meta last_export_hash), then call AutoImport. Import should be skipped because the file hash matches the export hash. Verify by checking that nodes were NOT reimported (no write transaction occurred).

TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenFileChangedExternally: Create a sync service, insert nodes, call AutoExport, then modify tasks.json externally (simulate git pull), then call AutoImport. Import SHOULD trigger because file hash differs from export hash. Verify by checking the imported data matches the modified file.

TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenNoExportHashExists: Fresh database with no last_export_hash in meta. Place a tasks.json file. AutoImport should trigger (fail-safe to current behavior).

TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenSiblingAgentExported: Agent A exports (sets meta hash). Agent B has not exported. Agent B runs AutoImport. Should skip because meta hash matches file hash (Agent A and B share the same DB).

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. In AutoExport (sync_service.go), after writing tasks.json and hash files, write the file hash to meta: store.WriteDB().ExecContext(ctx, INSERT OR REPLACE INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('last_export_hash', ?), fileHash)

2. In AutoImport (sync_service.go), after detecting file hash differs from stored hash, check meta: SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key = 'last_export_hash'. If file hash matches meta value, log at DEBUG level and return nil (skip import).

3. Fail-safe: if meta read fails or returns no row, proceed with import (current behavior).
